Details: Free chlorine can effectively inactivate S. typhi. The disinfection efficacy is dependent on contact time, chlorine dose, pH and temperature. A lab study in 1940s investigated the disinfection efficiency of free chlorine at various pH levels and two temperature ranges, from 2 to 5 °C and from 20 to 25 °C [960]. The study showed 100 percent inactivation of related E. typhosa using a free chlorine dose of 0.06 mg/L and a contact time of 3 minutes at pH 7.0 from 20 to 25 °C. Study results suggest disinfection efficiency decreases as pH increases and temperature decreases. A more recent study showed that 0.5 mg/L chlorine in tap water can achieve 100 percent S. typhi inactivation within 24 hours in the temperature range of -20 to 37 °C [958].
